intent: Add bulk course delete with selection mode and undo to the Courses page. Selection mode activates checkboxes on all course cards across grid/compact/list views. An action bar replaces the control bar with Select All, Deselect All, Delete Selected, and Cancel. Deletions run sequentially with undo toast (8s).

findings.title: Completion badge overlaps selection checkbox in grid card
findings.suggested_fix: Increase checkbox wrapper z-index to z-40 when a completion or progress badge is also rendered, or reposition the checkbox to top-3 right-3. The simplest fix: change the checkbox wrapper z-30 to z-40 in ImportedCourseCard.tsx. Alternatively, conditionally render the checkbox at a different position when completionPercent > 0.
findings.why_it_matters: The selection checkbox (line 363) and completion badge/progress ring (lines 376-388) both use absolute top-3 left-3 z-30. Since the completion badge renders after the checkbox in DOM order, the badge (bg-success with px-3 py-1) completely obscures the checkbox when both are visible. Users cannot select a completed or in-progress course by clicking the checkbox area; they must click the card body instead, which may be confusing.
evidence: ImportedCourseCard.tsx:363-373 renders checkbox at position top-3 left-3 z-30
ImportedCourseCard.tsx:376-388 renders completion badge at position top-3 left-3 z-30
Checkbox renders first in DOM, completion badge renders second thus overlays it
Semi-transparent bg-background/80 on checkbox wrapper is insufficient against bg-success badge

findings.title: Missing unit tests specified in implementation plan
findings.suggested_fix: Create the unit test files listed in the plan: src/app/components/figma/__tests__/ImportedCourseCard.test.tsx (checkbox render/no-render), src/pages/__tests__/Courses.test.tsx (selection mode state transitions), src/stores/__tests__/useCourseImportStore.test.ts (batch delete, undo, partial failure). Or mark the plan checkboxes as intentionally skipped with rationale.
findings.why_it_matters: The plan's Implementation Units specify 5 unit test files across Units 1-3. None were created. Only the E2E test covers the feature. Unit tests would exercise edge cases more thoroughly: checkbox rendering conditional on onToggleSelect prop, selection mode state management (Escape, Select All, Deselect All), store error handling (partial failures, total failures, rollback verification), and undo behavior (snapshot capture, re-queue syncableWrite). Without these, regressions in isolated logic are harder to catch.
evidence: Plan Unit 1 lists 3 component test files — none created
Plan Unit 2 lists 1 page test file — not created
Plan Unit 3 lists 1 store test file — not created

findings.title: Selection action bar lacks semantic grouping for accessibility
findings.suggested_fix: Add role="toolbar" and aria-label="Course selection" to the selection action bar div element for better screen reader context.
findings.why_it_matters: The action bar replaces the control bar when selection mode is active. While all buttons have text labels (providing basic accessibility), the grouping lacks semantic structure. A toolbar role would help screen reader users understand that these controls are a cohesive set of selection-mode actions.
evidence: Courses.tsx:583 renders div with data-testid='selection-action-bar' but no role or aria-label
Compare: the control bar it replaces also lacks a role — this is a style choice, not a regression

residual_risks: Undo restores course records but not child records (videos, PDFs) — documented in scope boundaries but could surprise users. Consider adding a toast note: 'Courses restored. Re-import videos to recover content.'

Sequential deletion of 100+ courses may take 5-10 seconds with no progress indicator beyond incremental card disappearance. The plan acknowledges this but it could degrade UX for power users with large libraries.

If user navigates away during batch deletion, in-flight deletions still complete in Dexie/sync queue, but the optimistic removal from Zustand persists. Remaining undeleted courses reappear on next loadImportedCourses(). Acceptable per scope boundaries.
learnings: The implementer correctly followed the established undo pattern from useAuthorStore.deleteAuthor (closure-capture -> optimistic remove -> toastWithUndo).

The implementer correctly used sequential iteration instead of Promise.allSettled to avoid Zustand state races, consistent with the lesson from docs/solutions/best-practices/paths-as-study-plan-implementation-lessons-2026-05-04.md.
The Select All implementation correctly uses sortedImportedCourses (filtered list) per R5 — confirming filter-aware selection.
testing_gaps: No unit tests for card components: checkbox conditional rendering, click propagation, and keyboard behavior.
No unit tests for Courses page: selection mode state transitions, Escape handling, Select All/Deselect All with filters.
No unit tests for store: removeImportedCourses with partial failure, undo callback, edge cases (empty array, all fail).
verdict_detail: Code is structurally sound and covers all 11 requirements. The two P2 findings (completion badge overlap, missing unit tests) should be addressed before merging. The overlap is a functional defect in selection mode UX. Missing unit tests represent incomplete implementation per the explicit plan.
